content.text: Odilitime clarified that the labs company is no longer operating but the ElizaOS open source project remains active. A community member who had been away asked about the project status and noted they could not run JavaScript on their current setup. Separately, Odilitime confirmed that Shaw has not launched any new token, addressing community speculation on the topic.

A community member raised concerns about whether the ElizaOS team had abandoned the ElizaOS token, noting that a wallet address starting with eliza on Solscan had sold all its tokens months ago. Odilitime responded that while the team's current focus is on waifu.fun, ElizaOS is not dead or abandoned, and pointed to a third-party project from the ElizaEco Fund building prediction game utility for ElizaOS token holders.

Odilitime clarified that plugin-solana has been moved into the main Eliza repo and is now available as plugin-wallet, with Solana-specific code located at plugins/plugin-wallet/src/chains/solana in the develop branch on GitHub. A developer also announced plans to build a plugin-kamino integration for ElizaOS, and Odilitime confirmed he had started some work on it for version 1.x and offered to share the code.

A community member named Doc, identifying as a pre-snapshot holder from August 2025, asked about manual migration help after missing the February 4th portal deadline. Odilitime confirmed that manual migration is no longer available and that the migration process is closed. A representative from SKALE Labs also reached out to Odilitime asking who to contact regarding a partnership opportunity.

Faouzi, founder of Arsenal Quant, introduced himself and described the Arsenal Decision Engine, a high-performance decision oracle for DeFi agents that integrates with elizaOS via MCP and uses L402 Lightning for payments. He expressed interest in connecting with other builders in the DeFAI space.

A developer in the coders channel shared experience building a multi-agent workflow platform using ElizaOS with specialized agents for research, data processing, content generation, and tool execution. Key challenges included context management, memory persistence, and recovery from failed actions across long-running workflows. The tech stack included ElizaOS, TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes, and OpenAI APIs.

Xpes shared observations from experimenting with AgentKey MCP inside an OpenClaw setup, noting the integration allowed an AI agent to pull live Twitter and Reddit discussions for real-time sentiment analysis and trend monitoring rather than relying on static summaries. He encountered some Windows-specific setup challenges but found the integration smoother than expected once configured, and noted the MCP tooling space is becoming increasingly interesting.

The Mantle Network Turing Test Hackathon was shared in the coders channel, described as the first onchain benchmark for agentic AI at scale with a 100,000 dollar prize pool across six tracks including AI Trading and Strategy, AI Alpha and Data, AI and RWA, Consumer and Viral DApps, AI DevTools, and Agentic Wallets and Economy. Submissions are open from May 1 through June 15, 2026.
